methods.description: This method involves editing, assembling, and aligning raw sequences to check for stop codons, ensuring data quality for further analysis.

steps.description: Raw sequences were edited and assembled in SeqMan (DNASTAR).
steps.searchable_summary: edit and assemble raw sequences using SeqMan

steps.description: Sequences were aligned using MUSCLE implemented in MEGA X to check stop codons.
steps.searchable_summary: align sequences using MUSCLE in MEGA X

methods.description: This method involves integrating public records of Tanytarsus from the Barcode of Life Data System (BOLD) into the current database.

steps.description: Search available public Tanytarsus records from China in the Barcode of Life Data System (BOLD).
Assign 298 records with COI barcode of Chinese Tanytarsus to the present database.
steps.searchable_summary: assign COI barcode records to database

methods.description: This method creates a distribution map of Tanytarsus records using R packages.

steps.description: Create a distribution map of the 298 records from China using the packages 'ggplot2' and 'mapdata' with R (version 3.5.2).
steps.searchable_summary: generate distribution map using ggplot2 and mapdata in R

methods.description: This method calculates sequence divergences using the Barcode Gap Analysis tool on BOLD to assess genetic distances.

steps.description: Use the 'Barcode Gap Analysis' tool on BOLD to calculate sequence divergences for the present dataset.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate sequence divergences using Barcode Gap Analysis on BOLD

methods.description: This method applies the BIN system on BOLD to cluster DNA barcodes and generate OTUs.

steps.description: Apply all 298 barcode sequences to the Barcode Index Number (BIN) system on BOLD.
steps.searchable_summary: apply barcode sequences to BIN system on BOLD

methods.description: This method constructs a neighbour joining tree using the Kimura 2-Parameter model to analyze genetic relationships.

steps.description: Construct a neighbour joining (NJ) tree based on the aligned dataset using the Kimura 2-Parameter (K2P) model with 1,000 bootstrap replicates and pairwise deletion in MEGA X.
steps.searchable_summary: construct NJ tree using K2P model in MEGA X
